text.title: Microservices Service Boundaries Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real microservice rollouts.

antipattern.symptom: Schema migration in service A breaks service B; deploys must be coordinated; outages during migrations.
antipattern.root-cause: Both services connect to the same DB; B reads A's tables.
antipattern.fix: Copy A's data to B's own DB via CDC; B reads from its local replica; remove cross-DB connections.

antipattern.symptom: One user request hits 12 services serially; p99 latency 4s; failures cascade.
antipattern.root-cause: Service boundary cut by technical layer (controller/service/repo) instead of business sub-domain.
antipattern.fix: Redraw boundary along bounded context; merge services that share heavy data flow.

antipattern.symptom: Producer adds field; consumer NPEs; integration tests miss the break.
antipattern.root-cause: Contract lives only in producer's code; no registry; no CI compatibility check.
antipattern.fix: Move contract to schema registry; CI validates every producer change against existing consumers.

antipattern.symptom: Two teams keep stepping on each other's commits to one service; design decisions deadlock.
antipattern.fix: Split the service along bounded contexts OR formally assign single ownership; the other team becomes consumer-only.
